title: Lighthouse Staircase Dream
raw_text: I climbed a spiral staircase inside a lighthouse while waves pounded the rocks below. At the top my grandmother lifted a brass lantern and said the sea was trying to teach me patience.

title: Glass Labyrinth Dream
raw_text: I kept returning to a recurring labyrinth made of glass walls and mirrors. A red thread was tied around my wrist, and every turn in the maze reflected a different version of me.

title: Hotel Corridor Labyrinth Dream
raw_text: In another dream the hotel corridors folded into a second labyrinth. I marked the carpet with chalk arrows, but the maze kept changing and I woke before I found the exit.

title: Shadow Riverbank Dream
raw_text: At the riverbank I faced a shadow encounter with a figure that had my posture but no face. The dark water moved around its feet, and a black dog watched without barking.

title: Moth Transformation Dream
raw_text: After the shadow encounter, my back opened and pale moth wings pushed through my skin. The transformation felt painful and calm at the same time, as if I had finally accepted an older fear.

title: Flooded Library Dream
raw_text: I waded through a flooded library where every shelf stood in dark water. The librarian told me the water symbolism mattered more than the books, because memory was moving before I was ready.

title: Train Station Farewell Dream
raw_text: I missed a late train while my mother stood on the platform holding one suitcase. She smiled like the goodbye had already happened many times, and the departure board kept changing its numbers.

title: Sky Bridge Flying Dream
raw_text: I stepped off a sky bridge and discovered I could fly over the city with cloth wings. The flying dream felt bright and effortless until I noticed that nobody below could look up.

title: Locked Rooms Childhood House Dream
raw_text: I returned to my childhood house and found three locked rooms behind the kitchen. Every key opened the wrong door, and the hallway smelled like wet wallpaper and dust.

title: Garden Snake Shedding Dream
raw_text: In the garden a green snake circled the peach tree and shed its skin in one clean ribbon. I wanted to touch the empty skin, but the snake stared at me as if it had already changed into something else.

title: Desert Well Dream
raw_text: I crossed a dry desert and climbed down into a dark well with a silver bucket. The descent into darkness felt deliberate, as if the bottom of the well held a message I had postponed hearing.

title: Mirror Double Dream
raw_text: My mirror double stepped out of the glass and copied every sentence one breath too late. When I touched its face, the reflection smiled before I did.

title: Whale Under Ice Dream
raw_text: A whale swam under a frozen lake and sang through the ice. I lay flat on the surface to listen, terrified that the ice would break open around my chest.

title: Classroom Examination Dream
raw_text: I sat in my childhood classroom taking an exam written in disappearing ink. Every time I answered one question, the teacher replaced it with a harder question I should have known.

title: White Wolf Guide Dream
raw_text: A white wolf guided me across a snowfield after I left a cave at dawn. The animal never looked back, but I understood that the guidance depended on following its pace exactly.

title: Ocean Cliff Lantern Dream
raw_text: I stood on an ocean cliff holding a storm lantern while waves rose high enough to touch my shoes. The water below felt dangerous, but the lantern stayed lit in the wind.

title: Bridge of Teeth Dream
raw_text: I crossed a bridge made of giant teeth over a silent quarry. Each step made the bridge chatter, and I woke when I realized the teeth were trying to memorize my weight.

title: Red Thread Reunion Dream
raw_text: A red thread looped through a crowded bazaar and pulled me toward my sister. I followed it past fruit stalls and bells until the thread wrapped around both of our wrists.

title: Harvest Moon Attic Dream
raw_text: Under a harvest moon I searched the attic for boxes marked with old family names. Dust kept falling from the rafters in silver sheets whenever I opened a lid.

title: Silent Observatory Dream
raw_text: I entered a silent observatory where every telescope pointed at the same missing star. The astronomer handed me a notebook full of constellations that ended one line too soon.
